steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that the narrator was frightened by the imagined one-legged seafaring man. The quotation must show the narrator's own emotional response — not a physical description of the sailor staying at the inn or neutral facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "How [that seafaring man] haunted my dreams, I need scarcely tell you. On stormy nights, when the wind shook the four corners of the house and the surf roared along the cove and up the cliffs, I would see him in a thousand forms, and with a thousand diabolical expressions.": directly conveys the narrator's fear and emotional turmoil. ✓
  • Choice B — "I remember [the sailor] as if it were yesterday, as he came plodding to the inn door, his sea chest following behind him in a hand-barrow.": describes the sailor's arrival — factual, no emotional response shown. ✗
  • Choice C — "[The sailor] was a very silent man by custom. All day he hung round the cove or upon the cliffs with a brass telescope.": describes the sailor's habits — factual, no emotional response shown. ✗
  • Choice D — "All the time he lived with us [the sailor] made no change whatever in his dress but to buy some stockings from a hawker. One of the [corners] of his hat having fallen down, he let it hang from that day forth, though it was a great annoyance when it blew.": describes the sailor's clothing — factual, no emotional response shown. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
